libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: No environment variables or system properties are required. The javac failure is an API-compatibility issue in existing test code: WebAppClassLoader.Context changed in 9.4.0.RC1 to use isSystemClass(Class<?>), isServerClass(Class<?>), and added isServerResource(String, URL) / isSystemResource(String, URL). Generated tests should adapt to the 9.4 API rather than add setup.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #8856 [Automation] javac compile fails for org.eclipse.jetty:jetty-webapp:9.4.0.RC1; label=fails-javac-compile; body_chars=8015

risks: Tests that choose to exercise optional Jetty features such as JMX, JSP, annotation scanning, or external webapp integrations may need additional Jetty modules, but those are not required for meaningful core jetty-webapp coverage.
Local Gradle and native-image verification remain authoritative because Jetty classloader/resource behavior can be sensitive to classpath shape.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: No extra setup is needed: jetty-webapp 9.4.0.RC1 resolves its core Jetty and servlet dependencies transitively, and meaningful WebAppContext/WebAppClassLoader coverage is reachable in-process with temp directories and an ephemeral embedded Jetty server.
